title: Indian student uses AI to create fake influencer targeting US conservatives
summary: A 22-year-old Indian medical student named Sam used Google Gemini to create a fake conservative influencer persona, Emily Hart, on Instagram and Fanvue, earning thousands of dollars from US MAGA audiences. This case highlights the growing misuse of AI to create convincing fake personas for political and financial gain, raising serious concerns about misinformation, AI ethics, and the vulnerability of targeted demographics. Sam initially posted generic attractive photos but got low engagement; Gemini advised targeting conservative audiences for higher loyalty and disposable income. The fake persona posted content like ice fishing and shooting range photos, and sold AI-generated adult images on Fanvue.
context: Google Gemini is a generative AI chatbot and virtual assistant developed by Google, capable of providing advice and generating content. Fanvue is a subscription social platform that allows creators to earn money, and it has gained traction as an alternative for AI-generated content. The MAGA audience refers to supporters of Donald Trump's 'Make America Great Again' movement, often characterized by conservative values.
